channel.title: Other on Shit PH Said, Mostly By Anwar
channel.description: Recent content in Other on Shit PH Said, Mostly By Anwar

item.title: [BM] Lesen Nadir Bumi Lynas Diperbaharui 10 Tahun — Sisa Radioaktif Masih Di Pahang
item.description:
Said by: Chang Lih Kang, Science Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 3 Mac 2026, Menteri Sains Chang Lih Kang mengesahkan pembaharuan lesen operasi Lynas Rare Earth selama 10 tahun, walaupun terdapat kebimbangan berterusan tentang penyimpanan sisa radioaktif. Greenpeace Malaysia memberi amaran bahawa negara berisiko menjadi “hab pencemaran.” Kumpulan alam sekitar mengatakan dakwaan kerajaan tentang pengekstrakan torium daripada sisa itu “tidak kukuh dari segi saintifik.” Hampir sejuta tan sisa radioaktif daripada operasi pemprosesan Lynas kekal tersimpan di Pahang, tanpa penyelesaian pelupusan kekal.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Pemprosesan nadir bumi adalah industri strategik yang kritikal dan loji Lynas Malaysia adalah yang terbesar di luar China. Tetapi nilai strategik tidak membenarkan pengabaian risiko alam sekitar dan kesihatan. Pembaharuan 10 tahun tanpa menyelesaikan persoalan pelupusan sisa bermakna Malaysia berkomitmen untuk sedekad lagi mengumpul sisa radioaktif tanpa pelan keluar. Kerajaan mengutamakan kedudukan geopolitik berbanding kebimbangan kesihatan dan alam sekitar penduduk Pahang.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: PH berkempen menentang Lynas dalam PRU14. DAP khususnya menjadikan Lynas isu alam sekitar utama. Fuziah Salleh — kini senator PH — membina kerjaya politiknya dengan menentang loji itu. Kini gabungan yang sama telah memperbaharui lesen untuk satu dekad penuh. Justifikasi — bahawa torium boleh diekstrak secara komersial daripada sisa — telah ditolak oleh saintis alam sekitar sebagai tidak terbukti secara teknikal pada skala. Ini kerajaan yang memilih pelaburan asing berbanding janji yang dibuat kepada komuniti yang diminta mengundi untuknya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Lynas telah menjadi beracun secara politik sejak loji Kuantan dibuka pada 2012. PH 1.0 mengenakan syarat ketat terhadap pemindahan sisa yang tidak pernah dikuatkuasakan. BN melanjutkan lesen sebelum PRU15. PH 2.0 kini melangkah lebih jauh dengan pembaharuan 10 tahun. Setiap kerajaan berjanji menyelesaikan isu sisa; tiada yang melakukannya. Coraknya: kempen menentang Lynas dalam pembangkang, memuaskan Lynas dalam kerajaan, harap pengundi lupa.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu berkempen menentang loji ini. Ahli kamu sendiri membina kerjaya menentangnya. Pangkalan alam sekitar kamu mengundi untuk kamu sebahagiannya kerana Lynas. Dan kini kamu beri mereka pembaharuan 10 tahun sementara hampir sejuta tan sisa radioaktif duduk di Pahang tanpa pelan pelupusan. Jika sisa boleh diproses, buktikan. Jika tidak boleh, jelaskan kenapa kamu memperbaharui. Pengundi kamu di Pahang berhak mendapat jawapan yang jujur.

Sumber: Greenpeace Malaysia | The Diplomat

item.title: Lynas Rare Earth License Renewed 10 Years — Radioactive Waste Still in Pahang
item.description:
Said by: Chang Lih Kang, Science Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 3 March 2026, Science Minister Chang Lih Kang confirmed the renewal of Lynas Rare Earth’s operating licence for 10 years, despite ongoing concerns about radioactive waste storage. Greenpeace Malaysia warned that the country risks becoming a “pollution hub.” Environmental groups said government claims about thorium extraction from the waste are “scientifically unsound.” Close to a million tonnes of radioactive waste from Lynas’s processing operations remain stored in Pahang, with no permanent disposal solution in place.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rare earth processing is a critical strategic industry, and Malaysia’s Lynas plant is the largest outside China. But strategic value doesn’t justify ignoring environmental and health risks. A 10-year renewal without resolving the waste disposal question means Malaysia committed to another decade of accumulating radioactive residue with no exit plan. The government is prioritising geopolitical positioning (reducing Western dependence on Chinese rare earths) over the health and environmental concerns of Pahang residents who live near the waste.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH campaigned against Lynas in GE14. DAP in particular made Lynas a flagship environmental issue. Fuziah Salleh — now a PH senator — built her political career on opposing the plant. Now the same coalition has renewed the licence for a full decade. The justification — that thorium can be commercially extracted from the waste — has been dismissed by environmental scientists as technically unproven at scale. This is a government choosing foreign investment over the promises it made to the communities it asked to vote for it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Lynas has been politically toxic since its Kuantan plant opened in 2012. PH 1.0 imposed strict conditions on waste removal that were never enforced. BN extended the licence before GE15. PH 2.0 has now gone further with a 10-year renewal. Every government promises to resolve the waste issue; none have. The pattern: campaign against Lynas in opposition, accommodate Lynas in government, hope voters forget.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You campaigned against this plant. Your own members built careers opposing it. Your environmental base voted for you partly because of Lynas. And now you’ve given them a 10-year renewal while nearly a million tonnes of radioactive waste sits in Pahang with no disposal plan. If the waste can be processed, prove it. If it can’t, explain why you renewed anyway. Your voters in Pahang deserve an honest answer, not strategic doublespeak.

Sources: Greenpeace Malaysia | The Diplomat

item.title: [BM] Kualiti Air Sungai Merosot — Lebih Banyak Sungai Tercemar Pada 2025 Berbanding 2024
item.description:
Said by: Natural Resources Ministry

Category: Other

Date: 2026-01-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Menurut data yang dikeluarkan Kementerian Sumber Asli pada lewat Januari 2026, kualiti air sungai Malaysia merosot tahun ke tahun. Sungai bersih menurun daripada 486 kepada 468. Sungai berkualiti “sederhana” meningkat daripada 161 kepada 171. Sungai “sedikit tercemar” melonjak daripada 25 kepada 33. Angka bergerak ke arah yang salah merentasi setiap kategori. Kemerosotan ini berlaku ketika kerajaan secara agresif mendorong pengembangan perlombongan nadir bumi — satu tapak di Perak sudah menukarkan sungai kepada warna biru akibat pelepasan perlombongan — dan terus mempromosikan Malaysia sebagai peneraju “ekonomi hijau.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Sungai ialah penunjuk awal bagi tadbir urus alam sekitar. Jika kualiti air merosot sementara kerajaan mendakwa kemajuan alam sekitar, dakwaan itu kosong. Ini berlaku di bawah kerajaan yang mengumumkan Pelan Ketahanan Iklim, menetapkan sasaran ekonomi hijau, dan meletakkan Malaysia sebagai peka alam sekitar di pentas dunia. Data berkata sebaliknya. Lebih banyak sungai tercemar bermakna sumber air minuman lebih buruk, ekosistem terdegradasi, kos rawatan air lebih tinggi, dan risiko kesihatan bagi komuniti yang tinggal berhampiran laluan air terjejas — terutamanya rakyat Malaysia luar bandar dan berpendapatan rendah.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Kerajaan bermegah tentang sasaran ekonomi hijau sambil mengetuai kemerosotan alam sekitar yang boleh diukur. Anda tidak boleh serentak mendorong pengembangan perlombongan nadir bumi secara agresif, meluluskan pembangunan pusat data yang membebankan sumber air, dan membiarkan piawaian pelepasan industri tidak dikuatkuasakan — kemudian mendakwa kepimpinan alam sekitar dengan muka serius. Data kualiti sungai bukan subjektif. Ia diukur. Ia diterbitkan. Dan ia menunjukkan kemerosotan. Jurang antara retorik alam sekitar kerajaan dan hasil alam sekitarnya bukan masalah komunikasi — ia kegagalan tadbir urus. Apabila sungai anda semakin kotor di bawah pengawasan anda, sasaran hijau anda adalah rekaan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Penguatkuasaan alam sekitar Malaysia lemah secara kronik merentasi pentadbiran. Pembuangan toksik Sg Kim Kim di Johor (2019) memasukkan ratusan ke hospital. Sungai Perak bertukar biru akibat perlombongan nadir bumi (2025). Banjir monsun tahunan — diburukkan oleh penyahutanan dan perancangan saliran yang lemah — memindahkan puluhan ribu. Jabatan Alam Sekitar secara konsisten kurang dibiayai dan kurang kakitangan. Di bawah BN, perlindungan alam sekitar dikorbankan untuk pembangunan. Di bawah PH, pengorbanan sama berterusan di bawah jenama yang lebih hijau. Coraknya bukan partisan: korek sekarang, bersih tak pernah.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Terbitkan pecahan sungai demi sungai. Namakan pencemar. Kuatkuasakan piawaian pelepasan dengan penalti sebenar — bukan surat amaran yang difailkan dan dilupakan. Jika perlombongan nadir bumi menukarkan sungai biru dan kualiti air merosot secara nasional, naratif “ekonomi hijau” anda adalah pembohongan yang disokong data kementerian anda sendiri. Kerajaan yang mengukur kemerosotan alam sekitar dan tidak berbuat apa-apa mengenainya bukan mentadbir — ia mendokumentasikan kegagalannya sendiri. Biayai Jabatan Alam Sekitar dengan sewajarnya. Kuatkuasakan piawaian pelepasan industri. Atau berhenti berpura-pura anda mengambil berat tentang alam sekitar.

Sumber: Asian News Network

item.title: River Water Quality Declining — More Polluted Rivers in 2025 Than 2024
item.description:
Said by: Natural Resources Ministry

Category: Other

Date: 2026-01-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to data released by the Natural Resources Ministry in late January 2026, Malaysia’s river water quality deteriorated year-over-year. Clean rivers dropped from 486 to 468. “Moderate” quality rivers rose from 161 to 171. “Slightly polluted” rivers jumped from 25 to 33. The numbers are moving in the wrong direction across every category. This decline occurred as the government aggressively pushed rare earth mining expansion — one site in Perak already turned a river visibly blue from mining discharges — and continued to promote Malaysia as a “green economy” leader.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rivers are the canary in the coal mine for environmental governance. If water quality declines while the government claims environmental progress, the claims are hollow. This is happening under a government that announced a Climate Resilience Plan, set green economy targets, and positioned Malaysia as environmentally conscious on the world stage. The data says otherwise. More polluted rivers mean worse drinking water sources, degraded ecosystems, higher water treatment costs, and health risks for communities living near affected waterways — disproportionately rural and lower-income Malaysians.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government boasts about green economy targets while presiding over measurable environmental degradation. You cannot simultaneously push aggressive rare earth mining expansion, approve data centre developments that strain water resources, and allow industrial discharge standards to go unenforced — then claim environmental leadership with a straight face. The river quality data is not subjective. It is measured. It is published. And it shows decline. The gap between the government’s environmental rhetoric and its environmental outcomes is not a communication problem — it is a governance failure. When your rivers are getting dirtier under your watch, your green targets are fiction.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s environmental enforcement has been chronically weak across administrations. The Sg Kim Kim toxic dumping in Johor (2019) hospitalised hundreds. The Perak River turned blue from rare earth mining (2025). Annual monsoon floods — worsened by deforestation and poor drainage planning — displace tens of thousands. The Department of Environment has been consistently underfunded and understaffed. Under BN, environmental protection was sacrificed for development. Under PH, the same sacrifice continues under a greener brand. The pattern is bipartisan: extract now, clean up never.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Publish the river-by-river breakdown. Name the polluters. Enforce discharge standards with real penalties — not warning letters that get filed and forgotten. If rare earth mining is turning rivers blue and water quality is declining nationally, your “green economy” narrative is a lie backed by your own ministry’s data. A government that measures environmental decline and does nothing about it is not governing — it is documenting its own failure. Fund the Department of Environment properly. Enforce industrial discharge standards. Or stop pretending you care about the environment.

Sources: Asian News Network

item.title: [BM] Rasuah Balak Sabah — Berbilion Pembalakan Haram Sementara Shamsul Iskandar Dapat Lesen Perlombongan Dari Pejabat PMX
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Groups / MACC Investigations

Category: Other

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Sabah, rumah kepada antara hutan hujan primer terakhir Malaysia, terus menghadapi pembalakan haram dan tidak mampan secara besar-besaran yang didorong oleh rasuah dalam peruntukan lesen. Krisis ini dibawa ke perhatian tajam apabila setiausaha politik kanan Anwar sendiri, Shamsul Iskandar, didakwa pada 4 Disember 2025 menerima rasuah berjumlah RM176,829 daripada seorang ahli perniagaan tempatan untuk memudahkan lesen perlombongan mineral di Sabah. Reuters melaporkan pertuduhan itu mendedahkan rasuah “yang berpunca terus daripada pejabat PM.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Hutan Sabah adalah antara yang paling kaya biodiversiti di Bumi, rumah kepada orang utan, gajah pigmi, dan beribu spesies endemik. Pembalakan haram dan korup bukan sahaja memusnahkan ekosistem yang tidak boleh diganti — ia menjejaskan mata pencarian komuniti orang asal, menyumbang kepada banjir dan hakisan tanah, merampas warisan semula jadi generasi akan datang, dan menyebabkan kerajaan negeri kerugian berbilion dalam hasil yang hilang.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Hakikat bahawa lesen perlombongan dan pembalakan didakwa dimudahkan melalui pejabat PM sendiri — melalui setiausaha politik kanannya — bermakna rasuah dalam peruntukan sumber asli menjangkau ke puncak tertinggi kerajaan Madani. Ini bukan pegawai nakal di daerah terpencil; ini rasuah sistematik dengan laluan terus ke Putrajaya. PH berjanji untuk melindungi alam sekitar Malaysia dan melawan rasuah. Sebaliknya, orang PH sendiri didakwa mengkomersialkan akses kepada sumber Sabah dari pejabat PM.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Rasuah balak Sabah sudah berdekad lamanya. Di bawah BN, konsesi pembalakan diagihkan sebagai naungan politik — paling terkenal di bawah bekas KM Musa Aman. Coraknya dwipartisan: sesiapa yang mengawal akses kepada sumber asli Sabah menggunakan akses itu untuk keuntungan politik dan peribadi. Kerajaan berbeza, rasuah sama, hutan yang sama terbakar.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Pembantu kamu sendiri didakwa menerima rasuah untuk lesen perlombongan Sabah. DARI PEJABAT KAMU. Hutan Sabah tidak perlukan satu lagi “pasukan petugas” atau “jawatankuasa khas.” Mereka perlukan kerajaan yang orangnya sendiri tidak memudahkan kemusnahan mereka. Terbitkan setiap lesen perlombongan dan pembalakan yang dikeluarkan semasa tempoh PH. Dedahkan siapa memohon, siapa meluluskan, dan siapa mendapat manfaat.

Sumber: Reuters

item.title: Sabah Timber Corruption — Billions in Illegal Logging While Shamsul Iskandar Got Mining Licences from PMX's Office
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Groups / MACC Investigations

Category: Other

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Sabah, home to some of Malaysia’s last remaining primary rainforests, continues to face massive illegal and unsustainable logging driven by corruption in licence allocation. The crisis was brought into sharp focus when Anwar’s own senior political secretary, Shamsul Iskandar, was charged on 4 December 2025 with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman for facilitating mineral mining licences in Sabah. Reuters reported the charges exposed corruption “emanating directly from the PM’s office.” The Shamsul case revealed how natural resource extraction in East Malaysia is systematically corrupted by political patronage — with the corruption pipeline running all the way to the Prime Minister’s doorstep.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Sabah’s forests are among the most biodiverse on Earth, home to orangutans, pygmy elephants, and thousands of endemic species. Illegal and corrupt logging doesn’t just destroy irreplaceable ecosystems — it undermines the livelihoods of indigenous communities, contributes to flooding and soil erosion, robs future generations of natural heritage, and costs the state government billions in lost revenue. The corruption-logging nexus turns public natural resources into private profit for politically connected actors.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The fact that mining and logging licences were allegedly being facilitated through the PM’s own office — through his senior political secretary — means corruption in natural resource allocation goes to the very top of the Madani government. This isn’t a rogue official in a remote district; this is systematic corruption with a direct line to Putrajaya. Shamsul had direct access to the PM. The businessman who allegedly bribed him knew that proximity to power translates to access to resources. PH promised to protect Malaysia’s environment and fight corruption. Instead, PH’s own people were allegedly monetising access to Sabah’s resources from the PM’s office.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Sabah’s timber corruption is decades old. Under BN, logging concessions were distributed as political patronage — most notoriously under former CM Musa Aman, who faced money laundering charges related to timber concessions before his case was dropped. The pattern is bipartisan: whoever controls access to Sabah’s natural resources uses that access for political and personal gain. Different government, same corruption, same forests burning. PH was supposed to break this cycle. The Shamsul charges show PH joined the cycle instead.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own aide was charged with taking bribes for Sabah mining licences. FROM YOUR OFFICE. The forests of Sabah don’t need another “task force” or “special committee.” They need a government whose own people aren’t facilitating their destruction. Publish every mining and logging licence issued during PH’s tenure. Disclose who applied, who approved, and who benefited. Commission an independent audit of Sabah’s natural resource allocation since 2022. Or admit that Madani’s green rhetoric is as hollow as the logs being illegally shipped out of Sabah’s ports.

Sources: Reuters

item.title: [BM] Banjir Perlis 2025 — 34,000 Dipindahkan, Ibu Baharu Terpaksa Lari Minggu Selepas Bersalin
item.description:
Said by: Government / NADMA

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-25

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir November 2025, banjir teruk melanda Malaysia utara — terutamanya Perlis, Kedah, dan Kelantan — memindahkan lebih 34,000 orang. Reuters menggambarkan pemandangan di mana air banjir mengubah seluruh komuniti menjadi apa yang kelihatan “seperti lautan.” Antara yang dipindahkan ialah seorang ibu baharu yang terpaksa melarikan diri hanya beberapa minggu selepas bersalin. Seorang pegawai polis hilang selepas kenderaan tergelincir ke dalam parit ribut berhampiran KLIA. Kerajaan mengumumkan Pelan Ketahanan Iklim sebagai respons, dan NADMA menyelaras operasi bantuan merentasi negeri-negeri terjejas.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Malaysia dilanda banjir besar setiap tahun semasa musim monsun. Setiap tahun, kerajaan mengumumkan pelan pemulihan, menggerakkan sumber, dan berjanji mitigasi lebih baik. Setiap tahun, komuniti yang sama dipindahkan semula. Banjir Perlis 2025 — 34,000 dipindahkan — berlaku hanya tiga tahun selepas banjir 2021 yang membunuh berpuluh dan memindahkan ratusan ribu. Kos kemanusiaan adalah nyata dan berulang: seorang ibu dengan bayi baharu melarikan diri dari rumah bukan statistik — ia kegagalan tadbir urus yang diukur dalam penderitaan manusia.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Pelan Ketahanan Iklim kerajaan diumumkan selepas banjir melanda — bukan sebelum. Itu bukan ketahanan; itu reaksi. Ketahanan bermakna infrastruktur yang mencegah banjir: kolam takungan, saliran dinaik taraf, kawalan pembangunan yang dikuatkuasakan, dan sistem amaran awal yang benar-benar berfungsi. Sebaliknya, Malaysia membelanjakan berbilion untuk bantuan banjir setiap tahun sambil terus meluluskan pembangunan di kawasan mudah banjir, menebang hutan tadahan, dan kurang melabur dalam infrastruktur saliran.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Banjir Disember 2021 membunuh lebih 50 orang dan memindahkan 400,000. Musim monsun 2022 dan 2023 membawa lebih banyak banjir ke kawasan yang sama. Selangor, Pahang, Kelantan, dan Perlis adalah zon banjir kekal — dan kerajaan tahu betul-betul kawasan mana yang akan banjir setiap tahun. Selepas bencana 2021, Anwar (ketika itu pembangkang) mengkritik kerajaan Ismail Sabri kerana pengurusan banjir yang reaktif. Kini dalam kerajaan, pendekatan PH sama: bertindak selepas air naik, umum pelan selepas kerosakan berlaku, dan janjikan penambahbaikan yang tidak pernah terwujud sebelum monsun seterusnya.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Anda mengkritik BN dan PN kerana pengurusan banjir reaktif. Tiga tahun kemudian, respons banjir anda sama: bertindak, gerakkan, janji, ulang. Terbitkan laporan perbelanjaan modal mitigasi banjir nasional yang menunjukkan apa yang sebenarnya dibina sejak PRU15. Kuatkuasakan kawalan pembangunan di zon mudah banjir. Biayai infrastruktur saliran sebelum monsun, bukan selepas. Seorang ibu baharu melarikan diri dari rumah dengan bayi beberapa minggu bukan perbuatan Tuhan — ia kegagalan dasar. Akui ia.

Sumber: Reuters | Malay Mail | Bernama

item.title: Perlis Floods 2025 — 34,000 Evacuated, New Mother Forced to Flee Weeks After Birth
item.description:
Said by: Government / NADMA

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late November 2025, severe flooding across northern Malaysia — particularly in Perlis, Kedah, and Kelantan — displaced over 34,000 people. Reuters described scenes where floodwaters turned entire communities into what looked “like an ocean.” Among those evacuated was a new mother forced to flee just weeks after giving birth. A police officer went missing after a vehicle skidded into a storm drain near KLIA. The government announced a Climate Resilience Plan in response, and NADMA coordinated relief operations across affected states.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Malaysia floods catastrophically every year during the monsoon season. Every year, the government announces recovery plans, mobilises resources, and promises better mitigation. Every year, the same communities are displaced again. The 2025 Perlis floods — 34,000 evacuated — came just three years after the 2021 floods that killed dozens and displaced hundreds of thousands. The human cost is real and recurring: a mother with a newborn fleeing her home is not a statistic — it’s a governance failure measured in human suffering.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government’s Climate Resilience Plan was announced after the floods hit — not before. That’s not resilience; that’s reaction. Resilience means infrastructure that prevents flooding: retention ponds, upgraded drainage, enforced development controls, and early warning systems that actually work. Instead, Malaysia spends billions on flood relief every year while continuing to approve development in flood-prone areas, deforest catchment zones, and under-invest in drainage infrastructure. The cycle is predictable because the government chooses not to break it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The December 2021 floods killed over 50 people and displaced 400,000. The 2022 and 2023 monsoon seasons brought more flooding to the same areas. Selangor, Pahang, Kelantan, and Perlis are perennial flood zones — and the government knows exactly which areas will flood every year. After the 2021 disaster, Anwar (then opposition) criticised the Ismail Sabri government for reactive flood management. Now in government, PH’s approach is identical: mobilise after the water rises, announce plans after the damage is done, and promise improvements that never materialise before the next monsoon.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You criticised BN and PN for reactive flood management. Three years later, your flood response is the same: react, mobilise, promise, repeat. Publish a national flood mitigation capital expenditure report showing what has actually been built since GE15. Enforce development controls in flood-prone zones. Fund drainage infrastructure before the monsoon, not after. A new mother fleeing her home with a newborn weeks after birth is not an act of God — it’s a policy failure. Own it.

Sources: Reuters | Malay Mail | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Sungai Perak Bertukar BIRU Akibat Perlombongan Nadir Bumi — Operasi Digantung
item.description:
Said by: Johari Abdul Ghani, Environment Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-20

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada November 2025, sebahagian Sungai Perak bertukar biru terang akibat pelepasan kimia daripada tapak perlombongan nadir bumi. Menteri Alam Sekitar Johari Abdul Ghani mengesahkan pencemaran itu dan memerintahkan operasi digantung sementara siasatan. Insiden ini berlaku ketika kerajaan Malaysia giat menggalakkan pengembangan nadir bumi — menandatangani perjanjian dengan AS dan meletakkan Malaysia sebagai pesaing kepada penguasaan China dalam mineral kritikal. Sungai biru itu menjadi simbol viral kos alam sekitar daripada usaha nadir bumi.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Malaysia bertaruh masa depan ekonominya pada mineral nadir bumi — komoditi strategik dalam perang teknologi AS-China. Tetapi pemprosesan nadir bumi menjana sisa toksik: thorium radioaktif, logam berat, dan produk sampingan kimia yang mencemari air, tanah, dan ekosistem. Insiden Sungai Perak mendedahkan jurang antara cita-cita nadir bumi kerajaan dan kapasiti penguatkuasaan alam sekitarnya. Jika satu tapak perlombongan boleh mengubah sungai menjadi biru tanpa hukuman, apa yang akan berlaku apabila Malaysia meningkatkan pengeluaran untuk menyaingi China?

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan menggantung operasi selepas sungai bertukar biru — bukan sebelum. Penguatkuasaan alam sekitar di Malaysia bersifat reaktif, bukan pencegahan. Kerajaan yang sama yang memperbaharui lesen Lynas selama 10 tahun (dengan sisa radioaktif masih disimpan di Pahang) kini menggalakkan lebih banyak perlombongan nadir bumi tanpa membuktikan ia mampu mengawal selia operasi sedia ada. Jawapan Johari — gantung dan siasat — adalah buku panduan Malaysia yang standard: bertindak selepas kerosakan, umum siasatan, dan harap kitaran berita berlalu.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Loji nadir bumi Lynas di Gebeng, Pahang telah menjadi kontroversi sejak 2012. Sisa radioaktif masih disimpan di tapak tanpa penyelesaian pelupusan tetap walaupun satu dekad janji. Tapak pemprosesan nadir bumi Bukit Merah di Perak (dikendalikan Mitsubishi pada 1980-an) meninggalkan warisan kadar kanser dan kecacatan kelahiran yang tinggi yang mengambil masa berdekad untuk dibersihkan. Sejarah nadir bumi Malaysia adalah sejarah kerosakan alam sekitar — dan kerajaan kini mengulangi corak itu pada skala lebih besar.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Anda mahu Malaysia menjadi kuasa besar nadir bumi. Tetapi anda tidak boleh menghalang satu lombong daripada mengubah sungai menjadi biru. Sebelum menandatangani lebih banyak perjanjian nadir bumi dengan Washington, buktikan anda boleh mengawal selia industri yang anda sudah ada. Terbitkan data pemantauan alam sekitar untuk setiap tapak nadir bumi aktif. Selesaikan isu sisa Lynas. Biayai audit alam sekitar bebas. Atau terus mengejar hasil nadir bumi sementara sungai bertukar warna — dan biarkan rakyat di hilir menanggung kos kesihatan.

Sumber: Reuters | FMT | Malay Mail

item.title: Perak River Turns BLUE from Rare Earth Mining — Operations Suspended
item.description:
Said by: Johari Abdul Ghani, Environment Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, a stretch of the Perak River turned bright blue due to chemical discharges from a rare earth mining site. Environment Minister Johari Abdul Ghani confirmed the contamination and ordered operations suspended pending investigation. The incident occurred as the Malaysian government was aggressively pushing rare earth expansion — signing deals with the US and positioning Malaysia as a rival to China’s dominance in critical minerals. The blue river became a viral symbol of the environmental cost of the rare earth push.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Malaysia is betting its economic future on rare earth minerals — a strategic commodity in the US-China tech war. But rare earth processing generates toxic waste: radioactive thorium, heavy metals, and chemical byproducts that contaminate water, soil, and ecosystems. The Perak River incident exposed the gap between the government’s rare earth ambitions and its environmental enforcement capacity. If a single mining site can turn a river blue with impunity, what happens when Malaysia scales up to rival China’s output? The environmental cost could be catastrophic.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government suspended operations after the river turned blue — not before. Environmental enforcement in Malaysia is reactive, not preventive. The same government that renewed Lynas’s licence for 10 years (with radioactive waste still stored in Pahang) is now pushing for more rare earth mining without demonstrating it can regulate the existing operations. Johari’s response — suspend and investigate — is the standard Malaysian playbook: act after the damage, announce an investigation, and hope the news cycle moves on. The river turned blue because enforcement failed. Suspension is not accountability.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The Lynas rare earth plant in Gebeng, Pahang has been controversial since 2012. Radioactive waste remains stored on-site with no permanent disposal solution despite a decade of promises. The Bukit Merah rare earth processing site in Perak (operated by Mitsubishi in the 1980s) left a legacy of elevated cancer rates and birth defects that took decades to clean up. Malaysia’s rare earth history is a history of environmental damage — and the government is now repeating the pattern at a larger scale.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You want Malaysia to be a rare earth superpower. But you can’t stop a single mine from turning a river blue. Before signing more rare earth deals with Washington, demonstrate that you can regulate the industry you already have. Publish environmental monitoring data for every active rare earth site. Resolve the Lynas waste issue. Fund independent environmental audits. Or keep chasing rare earth revenue while rivers change colour — and let the rakyat downstream pay the health costs.

Sources: Reuters | FMT | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Bajet Sukan Dipotong — Atlet Bergelut Sementara Kerajaan Belanja Berbilion Untuk GLC
item.description:
Said by: National Sports Council / Athletes

Category: Other

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Atlet Malaysia secara konsisten mengadu tentang pembiayaan yang tidak mencukupi, kemudahan latihan yang lemah, dan sistem sokongan yang tidak memadai, walaupun kerajaan menemui berbilion untuk penyelamatan GLC, pelantikan politik, dan faedah perkhidmatan awam. Bajet pembangunan sukan telah dihimpit apabila tekanan fiskal meningkat, menyerahkan atlet dalam sukan Olimpik dan Sukan Asia untuk bergantung kepada penajaan swasta atau pembiayaan sendiri.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Kemudahan latihan dalam banyak sukan sudah lapuk berbanding pesaing serantau seperti Thailand, Indonesia, dan Vietnam. Prestasi pasukan bola sepak kebangsaan telah menjadi sumber malu negara, dengan kemudahan dan pembangunan belia ketinggalan meskipun sukan ini mempunyai pengikut popular yang besar. Apabila atlet Malaysia berjaya — seperti yang kadangkala berlaku dalam badminton, terjun, atau berbasikal — kerajaan cepat menuntut kredit. Apabila mereka gagal, kekurangan pembiayaan sistematik yang menjadikan kegagalan tidak dapat dielakkan tidak diperiksa.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Perbezaan antara bajet JAKIM RM2.6 bilion untuk hal ehwal agama dan peruntukan yang jauh lebih kecil untuk pembangunan sukan memberitahu anda segala-galanya tentang keutamaan kerajaan. Malaysia bercita-cita menjadi negara sukan tetapi mendanainya seperti perkara sampingan.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Anda mahu pingat emas tetapi mendanai atlet seolah-olah mereka adalah baris perbelanjaan yang perlu diminimumkan. Anda mahu kebanggaan negara dalam sukan tetapi membelanjakan lebih banyak untuk birokrasi agama daripada pembangunan atletik. Pilih satu: sama ada danai sukan dengan sewajarnya atau berhenti berpura-pura peduli apabila pingat tidak datang.

Sumber

  • New Mandala — Pendidikan Tinggi

item.title: Sports Budget Slashed — Athletes Struggle While Government Spends Billions on GLCs
item.description:
Said by: National Sports Council / Athletes

Category: Other

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysian athletes consistently complain about inadequate funding, poor training facilities, and insufficient support systems, even as the government finds billions for GLC bailouts, political appointments, and civil service perks. The sports development budget has been squeezed as fiscal pressures mount, leaving athletes in Olympic and Asian Games sports to rely heavily on private sponsorship or self-funding.

🔥 Why It Matters

Training facilities in many sports are outdated compared to regional competitors like Thailand, Indonesia, and Vietnam. The national football team’s performance has been a particular source of national embarrassment, with facilities and youth development lagging despite the sport’s massive popular following. When Malaysian athletes succeed — as they occasionally do in badminton, diving, or cycling — the government is quick to claim credit. When they fail, the systemic underfunding that made failure inevitable goes unexamined.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The contrast between the RM2.6 billion JAKIM budget for religious affairs and the significantly smaller allocation for sports development tells you everything about government priorities. Malaysia aspires to be a sporting nation but funds it like an afterthought.

🎯 PH Callout

You want gold medals but fund athletes like they’re an expense line to be minimised. You want national pride in sports but spend more on religious bureaucracy than athletic development. Pick one: either fund sports properly or stop pretending to care when the medals don’t come.

Sources

  • New Mandala — Higher Education

item.title: [BM] Jerebu Merentas Sempadan Terus Kembali — Tindakan Kerajaan: 'Pantau Dan Harap Indonesia Bekerjasama'
item.description:
Said by: Environment Ministry / ASEAN Coordination

Category: Other

Date: 2025-09-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun Perjanjian ASEAN mengenai Pencemaran Jerebu Merentas Sempadan ditandatangani pada 2002 dan diratifikasi oleh semua negara anggota, Malaysia terus mengalami episod jerebu berkala yang disebabkan terutamanya oleh pembakaran pertanian di Indonesia. Respons kerajaan kekal sebahagian besarnya diplomatik — meminta kerjasama Indonesia dan mengeluarkan nasihat kesihatan — berbanding menangani sumbangan Malaysia sendiri melalui kebakaran tanah gambut domestik dan syarikat ladang milik Malaysia yang beroperasi di Indonesia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pemantauan kualiti udara semasa episod jerebu secara rutin menunjukkan bacaan IPU tidak sihat, menjejaskan kesihatan berjuta-juta rakyat Malaysia, menutup sekolah, dan mengurangkan produktiviti. Kos ekonomi jerebu berjumlah berbilion ringgit apabila mengambil kira penjagaan kesihatan, kehilangan produktiviti, kesan pelancongan, dan kesan kesihatan pernafasan jangka panjang. Perubahan iklim menjadikan keadaan lebih kondusif untuk kebakaran, namun penebangan hutan Malaysia sendiri berterusan pada kadar yang membimbangkan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Isu asas ialah keuntungan kelapa sawit diutamakan berbanding kualiti udara, dan budi bahasa diplomatik menghalang Malaysia daripada mempertanggungjawabkan syarikat Indonesia (dan milik Malaysia). Selepas dua dekad, masalah jerebu kekal pada dasarnya tidak ditangani. Kerajaan bersuara keras semasa musim jerebu dan senyap apabila langit cerah.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda menghadiri persidangan iklim antarabangsa dan membuat ikrar tentang kelestarian. Tetapi anda tidak akan berdepan dengan syarikat ladang — termasuk yang dimiliki Malaysia — yang menyebabkan jerebu yang rakyat anda sedut. Pemantauan bukan tadbir urus. Harapan bukan dasar. Selepas 20 tahun buku panduan yang sama, sama ada ubah pendekatan atau akui anda telah memilih keuntungan berbanding paru-paru.

Sumber

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Kegagalan Reformasi

item.title: Transboundary Haze Keeps Returning — Government's Response: 'Monitor and Hope Indonesia Cooperates'
item.description:
Said by: Environment Ministry / ASEAN Coordination

Category: Other

Date: 2025-09-01

📌 What Happened

Despite the ASEAN Agreement on Transboundary Haze Pollution signed in 2002 and ratified by all member states, Malaysia continues to suffer periodic haze episodes caused primarily by agricultural burning in Indonesia. The government’s response remains largely diplomatic — requesting Indonesian cooperation and issuing health advisories — rather than addressing Malaysia’s own contribution through domestic peatland fires and Malaysian-owned plantation companies operating in Indonesia.

🔥 Why It Matters

Air quality monitoring during haze episodes routinely shows unhealthy API readings, affecting millions of Malaysians’ health, shutting schools, and reducing productivity. The economic cost of haze runs into billions of ringgit when accounting for healthcare, lost productivity, tourism impacts, and long-term respiratory health effects. Climate change is making conditions more conducive to fires, yet Malaysia’s own deforestation continues at alarming rates.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The fundamental issue is that palm oil profits are prioritised over air quality, and diplomatic niceties prevent Malaysia from holding Indonesian (and Malaysian-owned) companies accountable. After two decades, the haze problem remains essentially unaddressed. The government talks tough during haze season and goes silent when the skies clear.

🎯 PH Callout

You attend international climate conferences and make pledges about sustainability. But you won’t confront the plantation companies — including Malaysian-owned ones — that cause the haze your rakyat breathes. Monitoring is not governance. Hope is not policy. After 20 years of the same playbook, either change the approach or admit you’ve chosen profits over lungs.

Sources

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Reform Failures

item.title: [BM] Krisis Pembuangan Haram — Malaysia Jadi Tong Sampah Dunia Dan Tak Mampu Bersihkan
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Enforcement / Local Councils

Category: Other

Date: 2025-06-01

Domestic illegal dumping remains rampant with local councils unable to enforce regulations. Recycling rates remain low despite targets. River pollution from waste dumping contributes to declining water quality nationwide.

item.title: Illegal Dumping Crisis — Malaysia Became the World's Rubbish Bin and Can't Clean Up
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Enforcement / Local Councils

Category: Other

Date: 2025-06-01

Domestic illegal dumping remains rampant with local councils unable to enforce regulations. Recycling rates remain low despite targets. River pollution from waste dumping contributes to declining water quality nationwide.

item.title: [BM] 37.1% Air Terawat Terbuang Setiap Hari — 7,195 Juta Liter Hilang, RM2B Ke Longkang
item.description:
Said by: SPAN / National Water Services Commission

Category: Other

Date: 2025-03-26

National NRW is 37.1% — over a third of treated water lost to leakage, 7,195 million litres wasted daily. Singapore’s rate is under 5%. Government spends billions on mega-projects but can’t keep water flowing through pipes.

item.title: 37.1% of Treated Water Wasted Daily — 7,195 Million Litres Lost, RM2B Down the Drain
item.description:
Said by: SPAN / National Water Services Commission

Category: Other

Date: 2025-03-26

National NRW is 37.1% — over a third of treated water lost to leakage, 7,195 million litres wasted daily. Singapore’s rate is under 5%. Government spends billions on mega-projects but can’t keep water flowing through pipes.

item.title: Climate Action Failure — Malaysia's Paris Agreement Pledges Are Fantasy, Emissions Keep Rising
item.description:
Said by: Climate Action Tracker / Environmental Groups

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite being a signatory to the Paris Agreement and making ambitious pledges on carbon emission reductions, Malaysia’s actual emissions trajectory continues upward. Deforestation continues, coal power plants remain operational, data centre energy consumption grows, and the government has offered no credible pathway to meeting its stated climate commitments.

🔥 Why It Matters

Climate change is already affecting Malaysia through more severe floods (2024 devastation), prolonged droughts, rising sea levels threatening coastal communities, and biodiversity loss. The country is simultaneously a victim and contributor — its palm oil industry and deforestation are significant global emission sources. Failure to act on climate isn’t just bad policy — it’s an existential threat to Malaysia’s future.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH’s 2022 manifesto explicitly promised environmentally friendly governance benchmarked against ‘International Best Practices.’ Instead, the government has approved massive data centres without environmental impact assessments, continued approving logging quotas that exceed sustainability levels, maintained coal-fired power generation, and offered no timeline for renewable energy transition. Anwar lectures the world about moral failing on Gaza while failing the planet on climate. The manifesto promise of strict logging quota enforcement is particularly laughable given continued deforestation.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia’s climate pledges have been consistently unmatched by action across all administrations. BN treated climate change as an inconvenience to economic growth. PH 1.0 made promises but collapsed before implementation. PH 2.0 is maintaining the tradition of ambitious targets and non-existent action. The Penang South Islands reclamation — a massive environmental destruction project — is being approved under a PH state government.

🎯 PH Callout

Your manifesto promised environmental governance benchmarked against international best practices. Your reality: continued deforestation, rivers turning blue from pollution, floods devastating communities annually, and climate pledges that are pure fiction. You can’t lecture the world about moral hypocrisy on Palestine while being morally bankrupt on the planet. The environment can’t vote — which is why you don’t care about it.

Sources

  • PH GE15 Manifesto (PDF)
  • FOE Malaysia — Penang Reclamation
  • East Asia Forum — Reform Aspirations

item.title: Palm Oil Deforestation Continues Unchecked — Environmental Pledges vs Plantation Profits
item.description:
Said by: Environmental NGOs / SAM

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite Malaysia’s commitments to international climate agreements and sustainable development goals, deforestation for palm oil plantations continues to degrade tropical rainforests among the oldest and most biodiverse on Earth. The Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) certification scheme has been criticised by international NGOs as inadequate greenwashing. Malaysia’s river water quality is declining — clean rivers dropped from 486 to 468 in 2025. The Perak River turned blue from rare earth mining discharge in November 2025. Endangered species including orangutans, pygmy elephants, and Malayan tigers face shrinking habitats.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Malaysia accounts for approximately 25% of global palm oil production, and the economic dependence on this commodity creates enormous political resistance to genuine environmental reform. While the rate of primary forest loss has slowed from its peak, secondary forest clearing and peatland conversion continue. The environmental cost — biodiversity loss, river pollution, carbon emissions from peatland drainage — is not counted in GDP figures but is borne by communities who depend on clean water, healthy forests, and functioning ecosystems.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The MSPO certification scheme is a sticker, not a standard. It allows continued environmental degradation while providing a veneer of sustainability that satisfies international buyers. The government talks about sustainability at COP summits and international conferences while approving new plantation developments at home. When your rivers turn blue from industrial discharge and your forests disappear for palm oil, no amount of international pledges will bring them back. Sustainability isn’t a speech — it’s a policy. Malaysia doesn’t have one.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, Malaysia’s deforestation rate was among the highest in the world. Mahathir’s era saw massive conversion of primary rainforest to plantations. Under PH 1.0, a moratorium on new oil palm licences was briefly discussed. Under PH 2.0, the MSPO was expanded but not strengthened. Each government pays lip service to environmental protection while protecting the plantation industry that funds political parties across the spectrum. The pattern is bipartisan: plantations are more politically valuable than forests.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You sign climate agreements and pose for photos at COP summits. Then you come home and approve more plantations. Your MSPO scheme is window dressing. Your rivers are getting dirtier. Your forests are getting smaller. Strengthen MSPO to meet international standards. Enforce illegal logging laws. Invest in downstream palm oil processing rather than acreage expansion. Or keep giving sustainability speeches while your country’s environmental foundation crumbles — and hope the world doesn’t notice the hypocrisy.

Sources: Asian News Network | FOE Malaysia

item.title: Penang South Islands: $14B Reclamation Project Destroys Fishing Grounds for 6,000 Fishers
item.description:
Said by: Penang State Government / SAM

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Penang South Islands (PSI) project — originally three artificial islands costing approximately $14 billion — threatens the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers, 51,184 tonnes of annual marine catch worth RM525 million, and 511 aquaculture farms generating RM1.67 billion per year. After sustained protests from fishing communities and environmental groups including Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM), the project was downsized to one island. However, construction proceeds, and the fundamental impact on fishing grounds and marine ecosystems remains.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Penang’s fishing communities have worked these waters for generations. The reclamation destroys their fishing grounds — permanently. No amount of compensation replaces a livelihood that sustains 6,000 families. The marine catch and aquaculture figures represent hundreds of millions in annual economic output that will be lost. The project benefits property developers; the cost is borne by fishing communities who have no political leverage against DAP’s state government.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP-led Penang has governed for over 16 years. The PSI project epitomises the contradiction at the heart of DAP’s governance: progressive rhetoric, developer-friendly reality. Reclaiming thousands of acres of sea to build luxury property while destroying the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers is not sustainable development — it is displacement development. The “downsizing” to one island was a PR concession, not a policy change. The fundamental trade-off — fishing communities sacrificed for developer profits — remains unchanged.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Land reclamation for development has been contentious in Penang for decades. The Gurney Drive reclamation, Tanjung Tokong development, and SRS Consortium controversies all followed the same pattern: developers propose, fishers protest, the state proceeds. DAP — which championed environmental protection in opposition — has greenlit more reclamation in Penang than BN ever did. The PSI project is the largest and most destructive yet.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You’re destroying the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers for a developer’s dream. RM525 million in annual marine catch. RM1.67 billion in aquaculture. Gone — permanently — so developers can sell luxury apartments on artificial islands. This is not development. It is the displacement of working communities for profit. If you can’t develop Penang without destroying the fishing industry, you can’t develop Penang.

Sources: SAM | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: [BM] Banjir 2024 Pindahkan Ratusan Ribu — Negeri-Negeri Utara Musnah Sekali Lagi
item.description:
Said by: ReliefWeb / East Asia Forum

Category: Other

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Disember 2024, banjir teruk di negeri-negeri utara memindahkan ratusan ribu rakyat Malaysia. Kelantan, Terengganu dan Pahang menanggung beban musim monsun. RM15 bilion telah diluluskan untuk mitigasi banjir selepas bencana dahsyat 2021, tetapi kemajuan sangat perlahan. Negeri-negeri yang sama yang dilanda banjir setiap tahun dilanda banjir semula, dengan komuniti yang sama menanggung kerugian yang sama. East Asia Forum mendokumentasikan bagaimana banjir 2024 mendedahkan kegagalan tadbir urus berterusan dalam kesiapsiagaan bencana dan pelaburan infrastruktur.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Negeri-negeri yang sama dilanda banjir berulang kali — Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, dan sebahagian Johor dan Selangor. Masalahnya bukan hujan. Masalahnya ialah kegagalan tadbir urus. Infrastruktur mitigasi banjir telah dijanjikan, dibiayai dan diumumkan di bawah setiap kerajaan. Pelaksanaan secara konsisten tidak mencukupi. Komuniti yang diberitahu “tidak lagi” selepas 2021 mendapati diri mereka di pusat pemindahan sekali lagi pada 2024. Apabila bencana yang sama menimpa orang yang sama di tempat yang sama tahun demi tahun, ia bukan bencana alam — ia pilihan dasar.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: RM15 bilion telah diluluskan untuk mitigasi banjir. Ke mana perginya? Projek mana yang siap? Mana yang ketinggalan jadual? Mana yang tidak pernah dimulakan? Kerajaan tidak menerbitkan laporan pelaksanaan yang komprehensif. Komuniti yang sepatutnya mempunyai saliran baharu, kolam tahanan dan projek pendalaman sungai masih menyusun beg pasir di rumah mereka setiap November. Wang itu ada. Projek telah diumumkan. Banjir datang juga. Jurang antara peruntukan dan pelaksanaan itu adalah ruang di mana kegagalan tadbir urus hidup.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Banjir 2021 membunuh 54 orang dan memindahkan lebih 400,000. Kerajaan meluluskan RM15 bilion untuk mitigasi. Banjir 2022 memindahkan puluhan ribu. Banjir 2023 memindahkan lebih ramai. Banjir 2024 memindahkan ratusan ribu. Setiap tahun, kerajaan mengumumkan bantuan, menjanjikan mitigasi, dan bergerak — sehingga monsun seterusnya. Kitaran ini berterusan di bawah BN, PN dan PH. Tiada kerajaan yang memecahkannya kerana memecahkannya memerlukan pelaburan infrastruktur yang berterusan dan mahal di kawasan yang tidak menjana pulangan politik yang sepadan dengan kos.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: RM15 bilion diluluskan. Ratusan ribu dipindahkan semula. Negeri-negeri yang sama. Komuniti yang sama. Corak yang sama. Terbitkan audit projek demi projek belanjawan mitigasi banjir. Tunjukkan projek mana yang siap, mana yang tertangguh, dan mana yang tidak pernah dimulakan. Pertanggungjawabkan kontraktor atas kelewatan. Posisikan sumber bantuan sebelum musim monsun. Atau terus melayan banjir tahunan sebagai kejutan tahunan — sementara rakyat terus kehilangan segala yang mereka miliki, setiap tahun, di tempat yang sama.

Sumber: ReliefWeb | East Asia Forum

item.title: 2024 Floods Displaced Hundreds of Thousands — Northern States Devastated Again
item.description:
Said by: ReliefWeb / East Asia Forum

Category: Other

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2024, severe flooding in northern states displaced hundreds of thousands of Malaysians. Kelantan, Terengganu, and Pahang bore the brunt of the monsoon season. RM15 billion had been approved for flood mitigation after the devastating 2021 disaster, but progress was glacial. The same states that flood every year flooded again, with the same communities bearing the same losses. East Asia Forum documented how the 2024 floods exposed continued governance failures in disaster preparedness and infrastructure investment.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The same states flood repeatedly — Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, and parts of Johor and Selangor. The problem is not the rain. The problem is governance failure. Flood mitigation infrastructure has been promised, funded, and announced under every government. Implementation consistently falls short. Communities that were told “never again” after 2021 found themselves in evacuation centres again in 2024. When the same disaster hits the same people in the same places year after year, it is not a natural disaster — it is a policy choice.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: RM15 billion was approved for flood mitigation. Where did it go? Which projects were completed? Which are behind schedule? Which were never started? The government has not published a comprehensive implementation report. Communities that should have new drainage, retention ponds, and river deepening projects are still sandbagging their homes every November. The money exists. The projects were announced. The floods came anyway. That gap between allocation and implementation is the space where governance failure lives.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The 2021 floods killed 54 people and displaced over 400,000. The government approved RM15 billion for mitigation. The 2022 floods displaced tens of thousands. The 2023 floods displaced more. The 2024 floods displaced hundreds of thousands. Each year, the government announces relief, promises mitigation, and moves on — until the next monsoon. The cycle has continued under BN, PN, and PH. No government has broken it because breaking it requires sustained, expensive infrastructure investment in areas that don’t generate political returns proportional to the cost.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: RM15 billion approved. Hundreds of thousands displaced again. The same states. The same communities. The same pattern. Publish a project-by-project audit of the flood mitigation budget. Show which projects were completed, which are delayed, and which were never started. Hold contractors accountable for delays. Pre-position relief resources before monsoon season. Or keep treating annual floods as annual surprises — while the rakyat keeps losing everything they own, every year, in the same places.

Sources: ReliefWeb | East Asia Forum

item.title: [BM] Banjir Terburuk Dalam Sedekad — 90,000 Dipindahkan, Cuti Menteri Dibekukan Terlambat
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2024-11-29

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir November 2024, Malaysia mengalami banjir terburuk dalam sedekad. Lebih 90,000 orang dipindahkan merentasi 9 negeri, dengan Kelantan, Terengganu dan Pahang paling teruk terjejas. Anwar membekukan cuti menteri — tetapi hanya selepas krisis sudah meningkat ke tahap kecemasan. Banjir monsun tahunan, yang berlaku dengan ketepatan jam setiap tahun, sekali lagi mendedahkan infrastruktur yang tidak mencukupi dan kesiapsiagaan bencana yang lemah meskipun berbilion diperuntukkan untuk mitigasi banjir selepas banjir dahsyat 2021.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Banjir monsun bukan kejutan. Ia berlaku setiap tahun, di negeri yang sama, pada bulan yang sama. Kerajaan yang tidak bersedia menghadapi banjir tahunan yang boleh dijangka bukan malang — ia tidak cekap. Angka 90,000 yang dipindahkan mewakili keluarga sebenar yang kehilangan rumah, harta benda dan mata pencarian. Membekukan cuti menteri selepas krisis meningkat adalah pengurusan reaktif, bukan kesiapsiagaan bencana. Kerajaan yang cekap akan menyiapkan sumber pra-posisi, pelan pemindahan dan penyelarasan menteri sebelum monsun tiba.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Selepas banjir dahsyat 2021 di bawah Ismail Sabri, kerajaan meluluskan RM15 bilion untuk mitigasi banjir. Di bawah PH, pelaksanaan sangat perlahan. Projek mitigasi ketinggalan jadual. Infrastruktur saliran di kawasan terdedah banjir masih tidak mencukupi. Komuniti yang sama yang dilanda banjir pada 2021, 2022 dan 2023 dilanda banjir semula pada 2024. Wang telah diluluskan. Projek telah diumumkan. Banjir datang juga. Apabila perbelanjaan mitigasi RM15 bilion tidak menghalang 90,000 pemindahan, sama ada wang itu disalah belanjakan atau projek tidak dibina. Kedua-duanya adalah kegagalan tadbir urus.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia dilanda banjir setiap tahun. Di bawah Najib, banjir 2014 memindahkan lebih 200,000 — dan Najib ditangkap bermain golf di Hawaii. Di bawah Ismail Sabri, banjir 2021 membunuh 54 orang dan memindahkan lebih 400,000. Di bawah PH, banjir 2024 memindahkan 90,000 — lebih baik daripada 2021 tetapi masih dahsyat bagi komuniti yang terjejas. Coraknya sama merentasi pentadbiran: persediaan yang tidak mencukupi, respons yang lambat, janji perbelanjaan mitigasi, pelaksanaan yang perlahan, dan komuniti yang sama dilanda banjir semula tahun depan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Banjir monsun boleh dijangka. Anda mempunyai setahun untuk bersiap sejak banjir tahun lepas. Anda mempunyai RM15 bilion yang diperuntukkan untuk mitigasi. Dan 90,000 orang masih dipindahkan. Membekukan cuti menteri selepas krisis melanda bukan pengurusan bencana — ia kawalan kerosakan. Terbitkan audit peruntukan mitigasi banjir RM15 bilion: berapa banyak yang dibelanjakan, untuk apa, dan mengapa ia tidak menghalang pemindahan tahun ini. Posisikan sumber sebelum monsun tahun depan. Atau terus bertindak balas terhadap bencana yang boleh dijangka dan tertanya-tanya mengapa rakyat tidak mempercayai anda untuk melindungi mereka.

Sumber: ReliefWeb | CNA | Bernama

item.title: Worst Floods in a Decade — 90,000 Displaced, Ministers' Leave Frozen Too Late
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2024-11-29

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late November 2024, Malaysia experienced its worst floods in a decade. Over 90,000 people were displaced across 9 states, with Kelantan, Terengganu, and Pahang worst affected. Anwar froze ministers’ leave — but only after the crisis had already escalated to emergency levels. The annual monsoon flooding, which occurs with clockwork regularity every year, once again exposed inadequate infrastructure and disaster preparedness despite billions allocated for flood mitigation after the catastrophic 2021 floods.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Monsoon floods are not surprises. They happen every year, in the same states, during the same months. A government that is caught unprepared by predictable annual flooding is not unlucky — it is incompetent. The 90,000 displaced figure represents real families losing homes, possessions, and livelihoods. Freezing ministers’ leave after the crisis escalates is reactive management, not disaster preparedness. A competent government would have had pre-positioned resources, evacuation plans, and ministerial coordination in place before the monsoon arrived.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: After the catastrophic 2021 floods under Ismail Sabri, the government approved RM15 billion for flood mitigation. Under PH, implementation has been glacial. Mitigation projects are behind schedule. Drainage infrastructure in flood-prone areas remains inadequate. The same communities that flooded in 2021, 2022, and 2023 flooded again in 2024. The money was approved. The projects were announced. The floods came anyway. When RM15 billion in mitigation spending doesn’t prevent 90,000 displacements, either the money was misspent or the projects weren’t built. Both are governance failures.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia floods every year. Under Najib, the 2014 floods displaced over 200,000 — and Najib was caught playing golf in Hawaii. Under Ismail Sabri, the 2021 floods killed 54 people and displaced over 400,000. Under PH, the 2024 floods displaced 90,000 — better than 2021 but still catastrophic for the affected communities. The pattern is identical across administrations: inadequate preparation, slow response, promises of mitigation spending, glacial implementation, and the same communities flooding again the next year.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Monsoon floods are predictable. You had a year to prepare since last year’s floods. You had RM15 billion allocated for mitigation. And 90,000 people were still displaced. Freezing ministers’ leave after the crisis hits is not disaster management — it’s damage control. Publish an audit of the RM15 billion flood mitigation allocation: how much was spent, on what, and why it didn’t prevent this year’s displacements. Pre-position resources before next year’s monsoon. Or keep reacting to predictable disasters and wondering why the rakyat doesn’t trust you to protect them.

Sources: ReliefWeb | CNA | Bernama
